introduction.p: According to a shipping company's regulations, the girth plus the length of a parcel they transport for their lowest rate may not exceed 120 inches, where by girth we mean the perimeter of one end.

description.p: ADD ALT TEXT TO THIS IMAGE

introduction.p: Suppose that we want to ship a parcel that has a square end of width x and an overall length of y, both measured in inches.

statement.p: Label the provided picture, using x for the length of each side of the square end, and y for the other edge of the package.

solution.p: The square end has side length x inches and the length of the package is y inches.

statement.p: How does the length plus girth of 120 inches result in an equation (often called a constraint equation) that relates x and y? Explain, and state the equation.

solution.p: The girth is the perimeter of the square end: 4x. The constraint is that girth plus length is at most 120 inches. To maximize volume we use the entire allowance, giving the constraint equation 4x + y = 120.

statement.p: Solve the equation you found in (b) for one of the variables present.
Hence determine the volume, V, of the package as a function of a single variable.

solution.p:
The volume of the rectangular box with square end of side x and length y is V = x^2 y. Substituting y = 120 - 4x:
V(x) = x^2(120 - 4x) = 4x^2(30 - x).

statement.p: What is the domain of the function V in the context of the physical setting of this problem? (Hint: neither x nor y can equal 0.)

solution.p: We need both x > 0 and y = 120 - 4x > 0, which gives x < 30. So the domain of V in context is (0, 30).
With a square end of side x and length y = 120 - 4x, the volume is V(x) = x^2(120 - 4x) = 4x^2(30-x) for x \in (0, 30).
